heaviest_rainfall.status: On 09 November 2025, a record rainfall of 47.89mm was measured, indicating an exceptionally wet day.  24.6mm per hour was recorded as falling at times during the 144.8 hour storm.

heaviest_rainrate.status: A torrential downpour occurred on 02 September 2025 when the rain reached a maximum intensity of 34.3mm per hour.  This happened during a 15.0 hour storm when 21.19mm of rain fell.

longest_rain.status: The record duration for continuous rain was set on 09 November 2025, the storm lasted 144.8 hours with a total precipitation of 47.89mm and it reached 24.6mm per hour at times.

most_lightning.status: The lightning strike count peaked at 225 on 28 May 2026, the highest since recording started on 11 April 2025.

storm.status: There are no active storms in the area.  The previous storm started on 18 July 2026 and lasted 1 day.

top_10_dry.status: It is currently one of the longest dry periods.  It is the ranked 1st since our records started in 23 September 2024.  There have been 23 consecutive days where it has been dry starting on 03 July 2026.

top_10_rainy.status: The longest rainy period started on 13 January 2026 and lasted for 22 days.

cold_snaps.status: Currently, the weather is stable with no cold snap in progress.  So far, 3 cold snaps have been recorded this year.  The average yearly count is 3.7.

extreme.status: The highest recorded temperature difference is 27.7°C from 24 April 2026 (when the temperature reached 23.2°C but fell to -4.5°C).

heatwaves.status: There is not a heatwave right now.  There have been 3 this year, compared to a usual average of 1.9.

tropical.status: The last tropical night was recorded on 28 June 2026.  The total number of occurrences since 1973 number is only 68.

average.status: The current average daily wind direction is south-westerly (208°).  In the UK, a south-westerly wind often means mild and wet weather.

gusts.status: The highest wind gust ever seen is 128.2 mph on 27 January 2026.

run.status: To date, the maximum wind run recorded is 1737 miles on 21 October 2012.

speed.status: The largest wind speed ever was on 13 October 2012 with a value of 93.7 mph.

top_10_windy.status: The longest windy period started on 11 October 2012 and lasted for 21 days.

summary.description:
Today, cooler for the feels like temperature (down 5.2°C) and outside temperature (down 4.0°C) and more moisture-laden outside humidity (up 14%).

The past week and month had observable variation in cloud levels (up 1812 m), feels like temperature (up 5.9°C), outside temperature (up 4.5°C), and outside humidity (down 12%) when compared to today.

Today’s average cloud levels is 1530.4m lower than yesterday, 1811.8m lower than the past week, 1393.9m lower than the past month.

The cloud levels for yesterday ranged from 1666m to 6280m and averaged 2420m, while the past week and month indicated warmer conditions.

summary.summary: Inside; hot for most of the day. Outside; you can expect comfortable, mild temperatures. Also, humidity should not be a concern and calm and still conditions are expected. Showers, mostly clear skies with high cloud, and it’s dim outside. Weather could become unsettled.

washing.phrase: The conditions are favourable for drying your laundry outside right now but, as always, monitor the situation for changes to the weather.
